per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah... I've also had a bit of a low-grade fever, usually in the evenings, it's not super high but it's there. And sometimes my body feels really hot and then I'll wake up drenched in sweat - it's pretty annoying, to be honest!

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I actually have been noticing that I get really hot and sweaty at night, sometimes so much so that my pajamas are soaked through. It's not just a little sweat, it's like my whole body is drenched. And yeah, I've also felt a bit lightheaded when I'm standing up or getting out of bed, but it usually goes away after I sit back down for a minute.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually been having a low-grade fever, usually around 100.8 degrees Fahrenheit, and it's usually worse in the evenings. Like I said earlier, sometimes my body feels really hot and then I'll wake up drenched in sweat. It's pretty annoying, to be honest!

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, since all of this started, my appetite has been off and I've just not felt like eating much of anything. Even when I am hungry, it's hard for me to focus on food because everything else feels so overwhelming. Usually, I'm a pretty healthy eater, trying to get in lots of fruits and veggies and whole grains, but lately, I've just been surviving on whatever is easy and convenient, which isn't great for me at all.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't think so... actually, one thing has been on my mind lately - my family's history of lung issues. With my grandmother passing away from lung cancer and my mom having asthma, it's hard not to worry about whether there might be something more serious going on with me right now. Does that sound irrational?
